card.intuition: Capturing the rendered output (usually HTML string or component tree) of a component and saving it to a file, then ensuring it doesn't change unexpectedly in the future.
card.analogy: A physical photo of your room: after you clean it up perfectly, you take a polaroid. A week later, you compare the room to the polaroid. If a shoe is on the bed, the 'test' fails and you have to decide if that's a mistake or if you meant to move the shoe.
when_to_use: Testing large, static data objects or config files
Ensuring a component's rendered HTML structure remains stable
Detecting unexpected changes in API responses
gotchas: Snapshots can be 'fragile': if you change one class name for styling, 50 tests might fail, leading to developers blindly pressing 'U' (update) without actually checking the diff
They don't test if the UI *works*, only if it *looks the same as before* in code

exercise.prompt: Identify the Jest function used for snapshot matching.
